page: Microsoft Azure E4-2s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.25 MB L2 cache, and a 50 MB L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 66 GB volume, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear tradeoff between high memory capacity and limited processing throughput. The single-core stress-ng performance score of 1346.69 falls into the bottom 25%, while the multi-core score of 1376.68 is in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show 85.4 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 9.64 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, both ranking in the bottom 10%. Computational benchmarks, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 871.0 and multi-core score of 1030.0, reflect the older processor architecture.

Due to the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring significant memory capacity without intensive CPU demands. It is suitable for small database operations, caching layers, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving. However, it is poorly suited for CPU-bound tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, or large-scale LLM inference, as evidenced by its low benchmark scores in those categories.
description: Microsoft Azure E4-2s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv3 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core, alongside 66 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this x86_64 instance exhibits a significant performance tradeoff: while memory capacity is high, benchmark results show low computational and memory bandwidth throughput, with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores falling in the bottom 10%. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as database caching, small database operations, and basic web serving, where resource density favors memory capacity over raw processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4-2s_v3 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 2 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, 32 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive workloads with modest processing requirements.
